blocks.content: Julian arrived at the Carteret County station at five to ten.

He stood in the doorway of the interview room for a moment before going in. Davenport was already seated — hat on the table, coffee in a styrofoam cup, notepad open. The recorder was centered between the two chairs. The chair across from Davenport had been placed slightly too far from the table. An old interrogator's trick — put the subject slightly off-balance, slightly removed from the table, slightly less in control of the space.
Julian moved the chair forward before sitting down.
Davenport watched him do it. Said nothing.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: Mr. Crane. Thanks for coming in.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Before we start — you spoke to the group this morning. They made the locked room argument. Door locked from inside, window is twenty feet, has to be natural.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Because it's the only argument available to them and it almost works. Almost. That's why you're still here instead of filing this as natural causes.
Davenport looked at him steadily. Then he clicked on the recorder.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: The beginning isn't Friday night. The beginning is months ago, when someone started planning this. But we'll get there.
